text: Real-time traffic speed prediction is an essential part of smart traffic system. However, current traffic speed models cannot well handle the characteristics of spatial and temporal heterogeneities contained in urban traffic data. In this paper, we propose Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) framework to improve the representations of traffic speed patterns in urban scenes by fully considering the heterogeneities of traffic data. The framework processes the urban traffic speed data based on the integrated temporal and spatial convolution networks, and adopts an adaptive graph augmentation method for both attribute and structure variables. Meanwhile, it includes two self-supervised learning tasks such as the soft-clustering-based spatial aware branch and contrastive learning-based temporal aware branch. Experiment results on four urban traffic speed prediction datasets validated the effectiveness of the proposed framework, and showed that the models achieved higher prediction accuracy than those of other existing methods. The ST-SSL framework helps to deal with the negative effects brought by shared parameter spaces in models, and the improvement strategy can be applied to deal with the characteristics of other complex spatio-temporal data.
